### Post by mayonesiac on 2006-07-03
so i installed my nvidia drivers using tselliots script, but cedega's 3d acceleration test still fails and i can't get any games to start. i 'm using a GeForce2 MX/MX 400 card with 32 mb. i know it's old and slow but 3d acceleration was supported way before that card came out (well maybe not "way back" but still). anyway, here's my xorg.conf

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon May 15 13:23:42 PDT 2006
```

### Post by tseliot on 2006-07-04
post the output of:
```
glxinfo | grep direct
```

and of:
```
dmesg | grep NVIDIA
```

---

### Post by mayonesiac on 2006-07-04
direct rendering: Yes


and



[17179586.936000] nvidia: module license 'NVIDIA' taints kernel.
[17179586.944000] NVRM: loading NVIDIA Linux x86 Kernel Module  1.0-8762  Mon May 15 13:06:38 PDT 2006

### Post by tseliot on 2006-07-04
It means that it works just fine. I had the same problem with Cedega but I could play games.

If you type this command you should be able to see a gear:
```
glxgears
```

---

### Post by mayonesiac on 2006-07-04
well i get about 700 fps with glxgears, is that high or low?

---

### Post by mayonesiac on 2006-07-04
ok i think there was a problem with cedega and not with the driver. i installed other games and they seem to work fine
